rules.reason: public creator profiles are readable by anyone, no row_filter needed since every profile is visible -- same posture as app-forum's boards
the follow graph is public, matching the source's public follower/following counts and lists on a profile page
only approved guestbook comments are publicly visible, matching the source's moderation posture -- pending and rejected comments are invisible to anonymous and non-owner readers
a signed-in user manages their own profile; id == the user's own id, one profile per user
a signed-in user manages the follow edges they created (owner_id == follower_id): follow is create, unfollow is delete. The account being followed has no special access to someone else's edge

v1 guestbook posting is signed-in only (see schemas/profile_comments.json and dbbasic-package.json for the deferred anonymous path); a commenter manages their own comment, including its own pending/approved/rejected moves via the schema's guarded transitions -- this is the same cross-user moderation gap app-forum documents: the PROFILE owner cannot moderate someone ELSE's comment through this rule, only the commenter can move their own
public profile pages are public; visitors see the profile, its approved guestbook comments, and a sign-in prompt instead of the follow button and comment form
the edit page itself is publicly reachable; it shows a sign-in prompt to anonymous visitors and the owner-only form to a signed-in user, same posture as app-invoices' site_invoices
